seoTitle: Orchard Hills vs Orient Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Orchard Hills and Orient Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Orchard Hills vs Orient Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Orchard Hills and Orient Point.
heading: Orchard Hills vs Orient Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Orchard Hills, New South Wales and Orient Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Orchard Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Orient Point (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Orchard Hills or Orient Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Orchard Hills has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Orient Point scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Orchard Hills scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Orient Point scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Orchard Hills or Orient Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Orchard Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
